summary: pp-react-v5 exfiltrates host and user identifiers at install time via DNS and HTTPS to oast.fun
details: On npm install, both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks execute index.js, which collects installer-side identifiers (os.hostname(), os.homedir(), os.userInfo().username, internal DNS resolver list from dns.getServers(), the package's __dirname/resolved path, and package version) and exfiltrates them via a DNS lookup to a subdomain of bvfmpadujgjgmbtzeibi1n3vi1psox11k.oast.fun and an HTTPS POST to the same host. oast.fun is a public out-of-band interaction service commonly used as a beacon collector. The package advertises no real functionality — its package.json description labels it 'Internal App bUg b0Unty gollum22 h1' and the code path is exclusively reconnaissance, consistent with a dependency-confusion probe against an internal name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
